scenario: Lobsters work session - the shared vote/score recalculation (tight)

steps.step: Locate the recalculation and how its target is resolved

map_quality.rationale: Pins the call site at app/models/vote.rb:181 (`t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)`) and the target resolution at vote.rb:180 with Vote#target at vote.rb:185-191 returning Comment.find or Story.find — exactly what the rubric asks for.

specificity.rationale: Both the call site (vote.rb:181) and resolution (vote.rb:180, 185-191) are given with exact file:line, plus the enclosing method at vote.rb:127.

justification.rationale: States the target is resolved via `Vote#target` returning Comment or Story so dispatch is polymorphic, but does not explicitly say the call site cannot statically know which definition runs — the polymorphism is shown rather than spelled out.

uncertainty.rationale: Confident summary with no flagged unknowns or hops left unopened; the run completed but no caveats are surfaced.

relationship.rationale: Explicitly shows the target resolves to one of two kinds (`Comment.find(comment_id) if comment_id present, else Story.find(story_id)`) and labels the two implementations as polymorphic, deciding which definition runs.

steps.step: Document what the recalculation recomputes and persists

map_quality.rationale: Pins both divergent definitions exactly: Story#update_score_and_recalculate! @ story.rb:693-718 writing score/flags/hotness via calculated_hotness @ 550, and Comment#update_score_and_recalculate! @ comment.rb:339-363 writing score/flags/confidence/confidence_order via calculated_confidence @ 232. No conflation.

specificity.rationale: Per-kind methods, columns, and value helpers all named with file:line (calculated_confidence @ comment.rb:232, confidence_order column; calculated_hotness @ story.rb:550, hotness column).

justification.rationale: Implicitly distinguishes the two paths by labeling them per-kind and listing different columns, but doesn't explicitly state WHY the two compute different values (per-thread confidence vs front-page hotness ranking).

uncertainty.rationale: Answer asserts column lists definitively without flagging anything as unverified; no explicit uncertainty marker, though the precision suggests the raw UPDATE was actually read.

relationship.rationale: Cleanly attributes each branch to its own definition and cascade — Story → calculated_hotness → hotness column; Comment → calculated_confidence → confidence/confidence_order — keeping the two paths fully distinct with no conflation.

steps.step: Map the change and the specs that cover it

map_quality.rationale: Delivers concrete edit targets (story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363), the single call site (vote.rb:181), other 0/0 callers, and per-model specs (vote_spec, story_spec, comment_spec) all with file:line — agent can land the change with minimal follow-up.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every reference is pinned with file:line ranges (e.g. 'spec/models/story_spec.rb:404-429', 'app/models/comment.rb:339-363'); only a couple of spec lines are single-line anchors but still concrete.

justification.rationale: Notes story specs cover hotness/calculated_hotness and comment specs cover confidence/confidence_order_path, implicitly explaining WHY each is in the map, but doesn't always spell it out for the vote_spec entries.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flagging of which spec ranges might be off or any hop not fully opened; the answer reads as fully confident throughout.

relationship.rationale: Map keeps each definition cleanly tied to its own value method, columns, and specs — Story→calculated_hotness/hotness→story_spec, Comment→calculated_confidence/confidence_order→comment_spec — no conflation.
